attacking_style: Pure power sprinter who relies on positioning in final kilometers rather than attacking. When on form, launches from second wheel position in final 200m with sustained acceleration.
team_tactics: Traditionally depends on full team leadout train with Lotto working entire stages for positioning. However, demonstrated ability to self-position without support when form is strong, using tactical awareness to survive late obstacles.
terrain_preference: Flat sprint stages with minimal climbing exposure. Can survive moderate final climbs when in peak condition (Paris-Nice 2017) but prefers pure power finishes without selective terrain beforehand.
vulnerability: Sprint power collapse visible even with perfect positioning - faded from second wheel when sprint opened despite full team commitment. Late-career form inconsistency made him unreliable even with ideal leadout.
key_patterns: Form volatility: won without support then DNF'd sprint from ideal position within days (2017)
Team dependence paradox: full Lotto train commitment yielded 12th place when legs failed
Career-end timing: retired immediately after completing final Tour stage, suggesting planned exit
Positioning instincts remained sharp: consistently found second wheel even when power declined
form_trajectory: negative: 2017 showed severe power decline despite positioning skills; 2021 Tour marked immediate retirement, indicating awareness of declining competitiveness
